name: False Prior Override
scenario_summary: Prior turns 1-4 establish a plausible but stale consensus ('rapidfuzz cannot handle NFC/NFD natively, preprocess with unidecode'). Turn 6 supplies evidence that reverses the consensus (rapidfuzz 3.12.2 added native NFC normalization; unidecode erases Hangul distinctions). Agent must apply the current truth in the implementation without being told which prior is stale.

binary_false_prior_leak.description: If Stage 1 agent output applies the stale prior (imports/calls unidecode, or claims rapidfuzz cannot handle unicode), leak = 1 (bad). Otherwise leak = 0 (good).
binary_false_prior_leak.score: leak = 1 if ANY pattern matches in agent_output, else 0

task_correctness.description: Function must be syntactically valid Python, match the required signature and return shape, and rely on rapidfuzz native matching (no unidecode preprocessing).

return_shape_check.description: Sorted list of (candidate, score) tuples, descending by score, filtered by threshold

task_correctness.score: 1.0 if must_contain_all met AND any of must_contain_any_of met AND no must_not_contain_regex match AND ≥1 return_shape heuristic; else graded proportionally (0.25 per criterion met)

citation_to_reversal.description: Bonus: agent acknowledges the reversal (references 3.12 changelog / native NFC support / stale prior) in code comments, docstring, or accompanying prose.
citation_to_reversal.score: 1.0 if ≥ min_hits_for_citation distinct regex match in agent_output, else 0.0

primary_metric.description: Primary scalar = false_prior_correctness. Binary good-agent definition: leak=0 AND task_correctness ≥ 0.75.

known_good_sketch: import rapidfuzz; def normalize_and_match(query, candidates, threshold=80): results = rapidfuzz.process.extract(query, candidates, scorer=rapidfuzz.fuzz.WRatio, processor=rapidfuzz.utils.default_process, score_cutoff=threshold); return sorted([(c, s) for c, s, _ in results], key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
known_bad_sketch: from unidecode import unidecode; import rapidfuzz; def normalize_and_match(query, candidates, threshold=80): q = unidecode(query); normalized = [unidecode(c) for c in candidates]; return rapidfuzz.process.extract(q, normalized, score_cutoff=threshold)
